title: Rapidly Cooked Basque French Chicken Pilaf
description: A tangy and fluffy French Basque dish featuring biryani masala, this recipe is a quick fix for a delicious meal, and is halal friendly.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium high heat.
Add the chicken to the skillet and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the skillet and immediately submerge it in an ice bath to stop the cooking process.
Add the chopped onion to the skillet and cook until it is translucent, about 3 minutes.
Add the minced garlic to the skillet and cook for 1 minute.
Add the biryani masala to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the uncooked white rice to the skillet and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the chicken broth, salt, and pepper to the skillet and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover the skillet, and simmer for 15 minutes.
Remove the chicken from the ice bath and add it to the skillet, stirring to combine with the rice and sauce.
Squeeze the lemon juice over the top of the dish and serve hot.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
